campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transportation funding investments for March 2026, adopt a new policy about how the city's property can be used, and discuss efforts to fill open city jobs and keep current employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and strategies to recruit and retain employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on a plan to clean up hazardous conditions on several properties that the County had identified. The council also adopted new policies on technology disruption and established a process for residents to request accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property abatement ordered by the County
New city technology disruption policy
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the town's finances from March, and authorize several agreements and contracts. Key decisions included accepting a donation of an automated external defibrillator (AED), approving a paving contract, and submitting grant applications for a statewide program.
keyTopics: AED (automated external defibrillator) donation accepted for town
O'Grady Paving construction contract approved
March 2026 financial and investment report reviewed
Statewide grant applications authorized
Fiscal Year 2026-2027 project list adopted

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize upcoming agendas and work plans. They cancelled their May 26 meeting, reviewed the June 2 draft agenda to add new items and set time limits for discussions, and updated their work plans for neighborhood services and government rules.
keyTopics: May 26 City Council meeting cancelled
June 2 draft agenda review and additions
Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan update
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan update
Event sponsorship approvals

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear public comments, and select new members for the Youth Advisory Committee for the 2026-27 fiscal year. The council also reviewed upcoming agendas for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee member selection for 2026-27
Public comment period
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Upcoming board and commission agendas

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council met on May 19, 2026, to conduct regular business and recognize two important observances: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month. The meeting included approval of April minutes and a formal salute to the flag.
keyTopics: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month recognition
Affordable Housing Month observance
Approval of April meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on May 19, 2026, to update the city's long-term health and safety plans through 2040 and to recognize the Public Works Department for their work on roads, trees, sustainability, and other infrastructure. The council also celebrated Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Public Works Department during Public Works Week
Celebration of Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month
Public Works divisions recognized: streets, sidewalks, trees, transportation, environmental programs, and facilities

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 20, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Charter Review Committee is holding in-person meetings on May 20, 2026, to discuss potential changes to the city's governing document. Residents can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and can submit written comments by email before the meeting.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meeting (in-person)
Remote participation options via Zoom
Public comment submission deadline: 10 a.m. day before meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held several public hearings on May 19, 2026, to address zoning changes, approve a development project, confirm property assessments, and permanently hire a new city manager. Residents had the opportunity to comment on new ordinances and development permits affecting the city.
keyTopics: New zoning ordinances being added to city code
Site development permit and map approval for a construction project
Property assessment confirmation and ordering
Permanent appointment of city manager Christian M. Curtis

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council met to discuss how Palo Alto can help residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances to comply with air quality regulations. The council reviewed proposed principles and programs to guide this residential electrification effort.
keyTopics: Residential electrification programs to meet air district requirements
Proposed principles for assisting residents with gas-to-electric transitions
Status update on the city's electrification efforts
